items.title: Wedding trip ends as 2 men nabbed at roadblock with suspected ketum
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Two men's plan to attend a wedding in Kelantan ended when they were detained at a roadblock in Lalang Pepuyu near Tanah Merah yesterday. The men were detained by the General Operations Force (GOF) 9th Battalion under Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan at about 10.30pm after a greenish liquid, suspected to be ketum juice, weighing about one litre and worth RM30, was found in the vehicle they were travelling in. GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said officers on duty at the Lalang Pepuyu roadblock spotted a Perodua Aruz carrying two men behaving suspiciously.

items.title: Anwar: DAP never disputes Malay rights and supports a government for all races
items.summary: MUAR, July 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim stressed that DAP has never disputed the rights of the Malays, thus refuting the narrative of certain quarters who are trying to portray the party as a racist organisation. Anwar, who is also the chairman of Pakatan Harapan (PH), said as proof, DAP has never objected to its intention to gazette 20.23 hectares of land in Bandar Malaysia as Malay Reserve Land. Poor Chinese should be helped, poor Indians should be helped, the majority of poor Malays should be helped,” he said at the Meeting Anwar Programme at a restaurant hall here today.

items.title: PM: New digital system in the works for Johor-Singapore border easing
items.summary: MUAR: A new digital system, to be launched jointly with Singapore in January next year, that is expected to ease congestion at the Johor-Singapore border. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, however, stopped short of revealing details of the initiative, saying doing so during the ongoing Johor election campaign could breach election regulations. He said traffic flow, border systems and congestion at the Johor-Singapore crossings had improved significantly over the past three years, adding that the new digital system would be launched together with Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong in January.

items.title: Thunderstorms and heavy rain to hit KL and several states until 4pm, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to hit Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and eight states until 4pm today. MetMalaysia, in a statement, said the warning covers Kelantan (Jeli, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang), Terengganu (Besut, Hulu Terengganu, Dungun and Kemaman), and Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Raub, Bentong, Pekan and Rompin). The warning also applies to Selangor, involving the districts of Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang.

items.title: Police: College skipped background check on employee in teen molest, sodomy case
items.summary: District police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said initial investigations found that the automotive college had failed to check whether the suspect had a criminal record. Sarudin said police would record statements from the college's management to assist in the investigation. "The suspect is currently remanded until July 11," he said, adding that police were also awaiting medical reports.

items.title: MOHE: International students form 12.6pc of IPT enrolment and do not reduce opportunities for Malaysians
items.summary: Data from the MyMoHES system shows total enrolment at 1,264,541 students, comprising 159,138 international students (12.6 per cent) and 1,105,403 Malaysian students (87.4 per cent). At postgraduate level, enrolment totals 114,601 students, comprising 75,929 Malaysians (66.26 per cent) and 38,672 international students (33.74 per cent). Of this, 145,727 (78 per cent) are local students and 41,097 (22 per cent) are international students.

items.title: Sultan Sallehuddin marks 84th birthday by conferring top honours on military and police chiefs
items.summary: Meanwhile, Kedah legislative assembly speaker Zubir Ahmad was conferred the Darjah Dato’ Setia Sultan Sallehuddin Kedah (D.S.S.S), which carries the title Dato’. Four individuals received the Darjah Dato’ Setia Diraja Kedah (D.S.D.K), which carries the title Dato’. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah was conferred the Darjah Dato’ Setia Pahlawan (D.D.S.P), which also carries the title Dato’.

items.title: Anwar-Onn Hafiz exchange just 'check and balance', not a quarrel, says Tok Mat
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan has dismissed suggestions that a public exchange between Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi amounted to a quarrel, describing it as a normal part of campaigning. I do not see it as a quarrel," said Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president. "Datuk Seri Najib's position is a federal matter.

items.title: Armed forces chief, IGP lead Kedah honours list
items.summary: Meanwhile, Kedah legislative assembly speaker Zubir Ahmad was conferred the Darjah Dato' Setia Sultan Sallehuddin Kedah (D.S.S.S), which carries the title Dato'. Four people received the Darjah Dato' Setia Diraja Kedah (D.S.D.K), which carries the title Dato'. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah was conferred the Darjah Dato' Setia Pahlawan (D.D.S.P), which also carries the title Dato'.

items.title: MPOB Research station to boost Melaka's palm oil industry: Ab Rauf
items.summary: JASIN: The Malaysian Palm Oil Board (MPOB) will build a strategic research station on a 40.47-hectare site in Seri Mendapat, Sungai Rambai, costing between RM20 million and RM25 million. Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh said the project, slated under the 13th Malaysia Plan, is aimed at boosting the state's palm oil industry. He spoke after launching the Jelajah Ketua Menteri Sayang Rakyat and Hello Komoditi 2026 events, held under the Sungai Rambai Wakil Rakyat Untuk Rakyat (WRUR) programme.

items.title: Strong westerly winds expected to hit Sabah, Sarawak and the peninsula from Wednesday, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) is forecasting a concentration of strong westerly winds that could potentially bring bad weather in several areas starting this Wednesday. The bad weather is also expected to hit the northern and eastern areas of Peninsular Malaysia on July 10 to 12, 2026. According to MetMalaysia, the situation could result in strong winds and rough seas in the South China Sea, Phuket waters and north of the Strait of Malacca.

items.title: 'Catastrophic' Super Typhoon Bavi set to hit US Pacific islands
items.summary: UNITED STATES: People in Guam and the Northern Marianas moved to emergency evacuation centers and made last-minute preparations on Sunday, hours before a "super typhoon" was projected to bulldoze through the US Pacific territories. The National Weather Service called the typhoon "very dangerous", warning of "tropical storm force" winds from Sunday afternoon or evening and "catastrophic" damage near the system's center later. It hurts," she told AFP.

items.title: MOF: Nearly 200,000 pickup and 4WD owners receive additional 100 litres under BUDI Diesel
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — Nearly 200,000 Malaysians who own diesel pickup trucks and 4WD vehicles have been approved for an additional 100 litres of subsidised diesel per month under the BUDI Madani Diesel programme, raising their monthly eligibility to 300 litres starting July. The ministry said recipients who use diesel vehicles owned by close family members may apply to transfer eligibility through the BUDI Madani portal, subject to verification. The ministry said BUDI Diesel uses MyKad verification to ensure subsidies are channelled only to eligible Malaysians, while non‑citizens and ineligible parties must purchase diesel at unsubsidised prices.

items.title: Sharon Teo banks on the late Salahuddin's legacy in Permas bid
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The experience she garnered working alongside the late Datuk Seri Salahuddin Ayub is the main strength for Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Sharon Teo Siew Hui in her bid to win the Permas seat in the 16th Johor election. She said her experience serving in the Pulai constituency under Salahuddin's leadership had shaped her to understand the people's needs. Teo said PH's Johor Untuk Semua (Johor for All) manifesto would form the basis of a specific manifesto for the Permas seat, scheduled to be announced tomorrow.

items.title: Youth must choose leaders who secure their future, not race politics, says Anwar
items.summary: MUAR, July 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on young people to reject divisive race-based politics and instead use the upcoming Johor state election to elect leaders committed to safeguarding their future. Anwar, who is also Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, said young voters should make their choice based on a leader’s ability to address issues that directly affect people’s lives, such as education, employment opportunities and the state’s development, rather than being swayed by racial sentiments. Also present were PH candidate for the Simpang Jeram state seat Nazri Abd Rahman and PH candidate for the Bukit Naning state seat Md Ysahrudin Kusni.

items.title: Johor campaign billboards torn down in Bukit Batu, Pakatan hopeful Arthur Chiong cries sabotage
items.summary: KULAI, July 5 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Bukit Batu state constituency Arthur Chiong Sen Sern expressed regret over the actions of irresponsible parties who committed sabotage and treacherous acts against his campaign materials. The Bukit Batu seat will witness a five-cornered contest involving Arthur (PH-PKR), R. Kumaran (BN-MIC), M. Premanand (Muda), G. Tamili (Bersama) as well as independent candidate Kamaruzaman Ali. N51 Bukit Batu: 49,963 voters R. Kumaran (BN-MIC) Kamaruzaman Ali (Independent/IND) G. Tamili (Together) Arthur Chiong Sen Sern (PH-PKR) M. Premanand (Muda) (2022: Chiong Sen Sern: PKR: Majority 137) — Bernama

items.title: 4 years jail, fine for labourer who kidnapped man over brother's debt
items.summary: Judge Zulkpli Abdullah imposed the sentence after Mohamad Syamsul Azroy Che Anuar pleaded guilty to charges of kidnapping the victim and stealing the victim's brother's car. On the first charge, Syamsul, along with three others still at large, was accused of kidnapping Mohd Shahrul Nizam Mohd Asri, 35, from a house in Tendong on the evening of June 6. The charge was framed under Section 365 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same code, which carries a maximum prison sentence of seven years and a fine.

items.title: TVET Commission Bill expected to reach Parliament by end of the year, says Zahid
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the commission would be set up to replace the National TVET Council as part of efforts to further strengthen the country’s TVET ecosystem. Ahmad Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, said extensive engagement sessions with various stakeholders were currently underway before the next steps could be taken. Also present were Deputy Rural and Regional Development Minister Datuk Rubiah Wang and Majlis Amanah Rakyat (MARA) chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.

items.title: AmBank wins Fitch outlook upgrade
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Fitch Ratings has revised its outlook on AmBank (M) Bhd's long-term issuer default rating (IDR) to positive from stable, while affirming the rating at 'BBB-'. "The positive outlook reflects our expectation that AmBank will continue to execute steadily on its business banking growth strategy, supporting its business profile and profitability, while maintaining asset quality performance," it said. It said AmBank's business profile has continued to strengthen, supported by consistent execution of its strategy.

items.title: MOH plans permanent appointments upon housemanship completion from 2028
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry aims to offer permanent appointments to medical graduates upon completion of housemanship from 2028 onwards, as part of efforts to resolve the long-standing contract doctor issue. Health Ministry secretary-general Datuk Seri Hasnol Zam Zam Ahmad said the ministry is currently negotiating with the government to implement the policy, while progressively absorbing existing contract medical officers into permanent positions. Hasnol said the ministry has sufficient vacancies to accommodate the appointments, although staffing movements such as resignations, transfers, further studies and spouse-placement requests continue to create operational gaps.

items.title: BN parties may swap seats for Negri Sembilan polls, says Tok Mat
items.summary: BN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said changing demographics required the coalition to adopt a bolder strategy to maximise its chances of victory. Mohamad, who is also the Umno deputy president, said previous election results would serve as a guide in shaping BN's strategy for the state polls. There could be seat swaps," he said, adding that some component parties enjoyed stronger grassroots support in certain constituencies, which could improve BN's chances of winning.

items.title: Johor polls: Anwar admits weaknesses, vows gradual improvement
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today admitted there are weaknesses in his administration but pledged gradual improvements for the benefit of the people. Anwar said the government remains committed to combating corruption and abuse of power, and ensuring that errant leaders are held accountable. "That power is your vote.

items.title: Visually impaired swimmer Faiq returns to Oceanman
items.summary: TUARAN: Visually impaired swimmer Faiq Adnan returned to the Oceanman World Series here for the second consecutive year. Last year, he also accompanied Faiq to the Oceanman World Final Championship at Kite Beach, Dubai. The two-day event featured the Oceanman (10km), Half Oceanman (5km), Sprint (2km), Oceankids (500m) and Oceanteams Relay (3x500m).

items.title: Stand with Onn Hafiz to continue developing Johor, Zahid tells youths
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Barisan Nasional (BN) hopes Johor's youths will continue to support caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, the coalition's poster boy for the July 11 state election. "I hope they will stand with Onn Hafiz to continue developing Johor. "Do you want to stand with Onn Hafiz to build Johor's future?

items.title: ‘I do not promise the moon and stars’: Pakatan hopeful for Bukit Naning pledges free ambulance, better internet and street lights
items.summary: MUAR, July 5 — “I do not promise the moon and stars, but I promise to work with the people to resolve outstanding issues,” said Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Bukit Naning state constituency, Md Ysahrudin Kusni. Holding to realistic principles, Md Ysahrudin, who was the former incumbent of the seat, placed the provision of free ambulance services as one of his five main pledges to ease the burden on residents if given a mandate in the Johor state election. Md Ysahrudin, 47, said the facility is very important due to Bukit Naning’s location being far from Muar Hospital and Batu Pahat Hospital, in addition to the constraints of ambulances at existing health clinics, which are often tied up with other emergency duties.

items.title: Langkawi not Phuket or Bali, says Sultan of Kedah
items.summary: ALOR STAR: The Sultan of Kedah, Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, will not compromise with any party that takes advantage of the situation by making statements or engaging in actions that could tarnish the image and reputation of Langkawi. His Royal Highness said Langkawi is a strategic asset of Kedah and the nation, which must be preserved and developed sustainably to continue driving the state's economic growth. Therefore, this valuable asset must be preserved and developed sustainably," His Highness said at the 2026 Kedah State Awards and Honours Investiture Ceremony at Istana Anak Bukit today.

items.title: Trump celebrates America's 250th birthday, blasts 'communists'
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump hailed America on its 250th birthday Saturday as the "crowning achievement" of human history, even as he used the event to renew his attack on domestic opponents he branded communists. and "Trump! Trump!"

items.title: Langkawi should forge its own identity, not copy Phuket or Bali, says Kedah Sultan
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, July 5 — The development of Langkawi Island should be shaped according to its own identity and character, without imitating or comparing itself to other tourist destinations such as Phuket, Thailand, or Bali, Indonesia, the Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, said. His Royal Highness said Langkawi Island was a strategic asset to Kedah and the nation, serving as a premier tourist destination and a key driver of the state’s economic growth. Also present were the Sultanah of Kedah, Sultanah Maliha Tengku Ariff; Raja Muda of Kedah Tengku Sarafudin Badlishah Sultan Sallehuddin; Raja Puan Muda of Kedah Che Puan Muda Zaheeda Mohamad Ariff; Tunku Mahkota of Kedah Tunku Shazuddin Ariff Sultan Sallehuddin; and Tunku Puan Mahkota of Kedah Che Puan Nur Julie Ariff.

items.title: Saudi non-oil sector growth accelerates in June as new orders hit four-month high, PMI shows
items.summary: RIYADH: Saudi Arabia's non-oil private sector strengthened its expansion in June, buoyed by the sharpest pick-up in new business in four months, though firms continued to grapple with steep cost pressures and weakening foreign demand, a survey showed on Sunday. Output growth held broadly steady, with roughly 18 per cent of surveyed firms reporting higher activity against just two per cent recording declines. "Stronger output and the fastest increase in new orders in four months suggest that business activity regained momentum towards the end of the second quarter," said Naif Al-Ghaith, chief economist at Riyad Bank.

items.title: King and PM extend birthday wishes to Kedah Sultan on his 84th birthday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today extended birthday greetings to the Sultan of Kedah, Al-Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, on the occasion of His Royal Highness’ 84th birthday. In a post on the official Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, His Majesty prayed that Sultan Sallehuddin would be blessed with happiness, good health and a long life by Allah SWT. Meanwhile, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim also conveyed his heartfelt birthday wishes to Sultan Sallehuddin.

items.title: Uber pauses Europe food delivery expansion as it pursues Delivery Hero deal
items.summary: NEW YORK: Uber has paused the majority of its planned food delivery expansion in Europe just months after announcing the plans as the ride-sharing company continues to pursue a takeover of Delivery Hero, the Financial Times reported on Sunday. Uber no longer plans to launch food delivery in five of the seven countries it had targeted for expansion this year, including Austria, Norway and Greece, the report said. Earlier in the year, Uber said it planned to expand its food delivery business into seven new European markets - Austria, Denmark, Finland, Norway, the Czech Republic, Greece and Romania.

items.title: OPEC+ tipped to raise quotas again as Middle East calms
items.summary: LONDON: Seven OPEC+ members are likely to raise oil production quotas on Sunday as Gulf countries are reeling from the Middle East war, analysts told AFP. "But for now, production is probably still below the group's targets," Staunovo told AFP. Between the first quarter of 2026 and May, combined production by Saudi Arabia, Iraq, and Kuwait – three of the seven countries raising their quotas – fell by some six million barrels per day, OPEC data have shown.

items.title: Man killed, another seriously injured after motorcycles collide at traffic‑light junction in Sungai Petani
items.summary: Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said initial investigations showed the crash involved two motorcycles at the junction. Police said the victim’s body was sent to the Forensic Unit of Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital for a post‑mortem and both motorcycles were taken to the police station to assist investigations. He urged witnesses or anyone with information to contact the Kuala Muda district police traffic investigation officer or visit the nearest police station.

items.title: TVET Commission establishment to be finalised by end 2026
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: The establishment of the National Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Commission is expected to be finalised by the end of this year. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said engagement sessions on the commission's establishment were ongoing. Zahid, who is also the rural and regional development minister, said policy approval for the commission had already been obtained.

items.title: Syariah Court postpones trial of 14 men charged with dressing as women
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Syariah Lower Court today postponed the trial of 14 men charged with dressing as women at an apartment in Jalan Mahmood, here, last year. Syarie Judge Wan Mohd Firdaus Wan Ibrahim fixed Sept 20 for the continuation of the trial. The 14 men are represented by lawyers Muhammed Syarafi Mohd Salleh, Mohd Faqqhuruddin Mohd Nasir and Muhammad Amirul Ashraf A. Ghani, while the prosecution is led by Kelantan Syarie prosecuting officer Syafiqah Mat Husin.

items.title: From dondang sayang to silat, eight Malaysian heritage arts now recognised by Unesco
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH, July 5 — Eight Malaysian cultural arts have received recognition from the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organisation (Unesco) since 2014, reflecting international appreciation of the country’s heritage. National Department for Culture and Arts (JKKN) director-general Mohd Amran Mohd Haris said the achievement was the result of close cooperation between the National Heritage Department and JKKN in preserving and elevating traditional arts on the global stage. He said this helps preserve traditional arts while ensuring they are practised appropriately.

items.title: Malaysia’s first World Rural Development Day highlights efforts to uplift rural communities, says DPM Zahid
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 5 — Malaysia will celebrate the World Rural Development Day (WRDD) for the first time at Tun Abdul Razak Stadium, Jengka near Maran, tomorrow. Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Rural and Regional Development Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi is scheduled to officiate the celebration. The Ministry of Rural and Regional Development (KKDW) announced in a post on its Facebook page that the celebration will emphasise three main thrusts, namely community innovation, rural digitization and rural entrepreneur development.

items.title: Anwar urges Johor voters to give PH a chance
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Johor voters to give the coalition a chance to govern the state, citing unresolved public issues under the Barisan Nasional (BN) administration. Anwar said the BN-led state government had neglected the people's interests, particularly over rising housing costs. "We manage the federal government, and we look after their (Johor) government.

items.title: [UPDATED] PM: Govt to boost support for AIM to reach more needy groups
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: The government is increasing support for Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia (AIM) to ensure no group, especially those in rural areas, is left out, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. "People often talk about helping the rakyat. Anwar, who is also finance minister, said public support for the unity government would be channelled back to the people through programmes under AIM.

items.title: Synthetic drugs fuelling mental health crisis among young Malaysians
items.summary: LETTER: I am concerned by the National Anti-Drug Agency (Nada) finding that synthetic drugs now make up 73 per cent of drug abuse cases in Malaysia, with more young users experiencing mental health problems, particularly through vape misuse. Synthetic drugs are no longer merely a drug abuse problem. They have evolved into a serious public health, mental health and social crisis.

items.title: Sec-gen says Pakatan’s seat-by-seat strategy is driving support in Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 5— The burgeoning support for Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the Johor state election campaign is driven by the effectiveness of its campaign strategy. PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said PH is implementing its campaign strategy by classifying the 56 state seats being contested according to priority levels based on current support trends on the ground. Saifuddin Nasution, who is also PKR’s joint election director, said the increase in support for PH was also aided by the opposition’s political statements that gave PH an advantage.

items.title: Johari: Political stability key to attracting investments, Maju Johor 2030
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Maju Johor 2030 agenda, which promises development and thousands of jobs, will not be achieved without a stable government with a clear mandate, says Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani. Johari, who is the investment, trade and industry minister, said political stability was the key to attracting foreign investment into the state. Johari said Johor's strong infrastructure was another advantage in attracting foreign investors, particularly those from Singapore.

items.title: Stumping for Pakatan in Johor, Anwar says rivals joined forces against him because his govt is tough on corruption
items.summary: They know we safeguard everything,” he said while speaking at the PMX Meet-and-Greet programme for the Senggarang state constituency in conjunction with the Johor state election here today. Also present was PH Communications director, who is also Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. He said that despite making numerous visits to Johor as Prime Minister, he had never taken any land, projects or shares in the state.

items.title: King extends birthday greetings to Sultan of Kedah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today extended birthday greetings to the Sultan of Kedah, Al-Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, on the occasion of His Royal Highness' 84th birthday. In a post on the official Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, His Majesty prayed that Sultan Sallehuddin would be blessed with happiness, good health and a long life by Allah SWT. Meanwhile, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim also conveyed his heartfelt birthday wishes to Sultan Sallehuddin.

items.title: Police obtain four‑day remand for 10 teens over Merotai Besar assault on student heading to Friday prayers
items.summary: The remand order, effective today until Wednesday, was issued by Magistrate Don Stiwin Malanjum to allow police to continue investigations under Section 147 of the Penal Code, according to Harian Metro . A police team from the Tawau District Police Headquarters and Bombalai Police Station arrested the suspects, aged 14 to 17, at 2pm yesterday. Police said the incident gained widespread attention after a video lasting nearly two minutes showing the assault went viral on social media.

items.title: Johor polls: Young voters remain Pas' biggest challenge, says Tuan Ibrahim
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Young voters remain Pas' biggest challenge as it seeks to broaden its support ahead of the Johor state election, says deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man. This is our challenge," said the Perikatan Nasional deputy chairman. Tuan Ibrahim said Pas has also become more experienced in fielding candidates outside their home states, with selections based on the party's overall electoral strategy rather than geographical origin.

items.title: Anwar: Govt will continue investing in Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia to reach those still missing out
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the move aims to ensure no group, especially in rural areas, is left out of govt initiatives. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the AIM microfinancing scheme is among the best initiatives of its kind globally and is difficult to match, including by Bangladesh’s Grameen Bank. He said while microfinancing models exist worldwide, AIM stands out for its significantly lower, borrower-friendly interest rates.

items.title: Nur Jazlan: No Pas-BN agreement in Johor, just a common opponent
items.summary: Nur Jazlan said many people had forgotten that Pas and its now-fierce rival DAP were once coalition partners. "So after Pas' relationship with DAP, and later PKR, broke down, they came to understand DAP's character," he said. Nur Jazlan said the BN government needed another five-year mandate to address these issues through continued infrastructure investment.

items.title: First batch of Year 4 pupils set for Malaysia’s new Learning Matrix test this Oct
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 5 — Around 400,000 Year 4 pupils are expected to sit for the Year 4 Learning Matrix, the first cohort of the new assessment system, scheduled over three days from Oct 6 to 8. Education Ministry Examinations Board (LP) director Ab Aziz Mamat said the assessment will be conducted simultaneously nationwide, with each subject tested in a written paper lasting one hour and 15 minutes. Ab Aziz said the assessment instruments are developed by the LP together with teachers involved in classroom teaching, based on the Curriculum and Assessment Standard Document covering Year 1 to Year 4 content.

items.title: Two dead, four injured after SUV slams into Pasir Puteh school surau in Kelantan
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 5 — Two Indonesian women died after the sports utility vehicle (SUV) they were travelling in with four other individuals is believed to have skidded before crashing into a surau at Sekolah Kebangsaan Bukit Jawa 2 on Jalan Selising-Bukit Merbau, Pasir Puteh, early this morning. Pasir Puteh Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) chief Azhar Helmi Mustofar said his department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 6.34 am before mobilising seven members from Pasir Puteh station and six members from Ketereh BBP with two engines to the location. He said upon arrival, firefighters found the SUV had skidded before crashing into a surau located on the side of the road.

items.title: Johor election: Nur Hafiz pledges yearly report card if elected Machap rep
items.summary: KLUANG: Pakatan Harapan candidate for Machap, Nur Hafiz Roslan, has pledged to publish a yearly performance report card if elected. The Amanah Youth executive member said the move is part of his "people-first" administration, which focuses on healthcare access, rural infrastructure gaps and long-standing Felda housing issues. Nur Hafiz said his campaign is rooted in direct engagement with residents on the ground, particularly elderly voters and Felda communities who, he claimed, continue to face unresolved structural challenges despite past interventions.

items.title: Immigration detains man linked to viral post alleging possession of both Malaysian and Bangladeshi passports
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — The Immigration Department of Malaysia has detained a Bangladeshi man in Kuantan after a viral social media post allegedly showed him holding both a Malaysian international passport and a Bangladeshi passport, even though Malaysia does not permit dual citizenship. Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said a joint task force comprising its Pahang office and the National Registration Department Pahang located the man inside a container at Jalan Kuantan Bandar Damansara on July 4. “The team found a Bangladeshi man inside the container,” he said, adding that officers also discovered two expired Malaysian international passports and a Seafarer’s Service Book belonging to a local citizen.

items.title: PM’s visit boosts morale of Pakatan campaign machinery in Machap and Layang‑Layang, say candidates
items.summary: KLUANG, July 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s presence on the ninth day of campaigning for the Johor state election has provided a significant morale boost to the Pakatan Harapan (PH) campaign machinery, particularly in the Machap and Layang-Layang state constituencies, as they intensify efforts ahead of polling day on July 11. PH candidate for the Machap state seat, Nur Hafiz Roslan, said the visit by the PH chairman not only motivated the candidates and campaign workers but also conveyed a clear message that every effort throughout the campaign should be centred on serving the people. Meanwhile, Guna said the Prime Minister’s visit had greatly helped his efforts to engage more closely with the local community.

items.title: Driver killed after lorry plunges 150 feet into ravine near Pos Blau–Lojing
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, July 5 — A lorry driver was killed after the 10-tonne lorry he was driving plunged into a 150-foot ravine near the Pos Blau-Lojing Bridge here yesterday. Gua Musang Fire and Rescue Station chief Asst Senior Fire Supt Muhammad Azrul Izzham Zulkifli said a firefighting team from his station reached the location, almost 98 kilometres away at 1.59 pm after receiving a call about the crash at 11.58am. “The team found that a 10-tonne lorry had plunged into the ravine near an orchard.

items.title: Mother arrested in Kajang after viral video of children leaning out of moving car’s sunroof on Grand Saga Highway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — Police have arrested the mother of two children who were filmed sticking their heads out of a moving car’s sunroof. Kajang District Police chief ACP Naazron Abdul Yusof said the 30-year-old woman was detained yesterday after she came to the Kajang District Police Headquarters to give her statement. Naazron said police had detected a viral video showing the two children, aged four and six, on social media.

items.title: Fahmi inspects Bukit Naning coverage issues, calls on telco CEOs to witness rural connectivity gaps first‑hand
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 5 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has described telecommunications companies as still being lax in addressing internet coverage issues in rural areas despite having sufficient data to identify problem locations. He said he would summon all telecommunications companies together with the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to seek explanations and expedite improvements in areas still facing internet connectivity issues. He said MCMC would issue a notice to telecommunications companies to immediately carry out network optimisation works, as there was already a telecommunications tower in Bukit Naning, but its signal still failed to cover Kampung Parit Jepon and the surrounding areas.t Apart from optimising the existing network, Fahmi said plans to build a new telecommunications tower would also be considered to improve coverage in the Batu 14, Batu 15 and Batu 16 areas.

items.title: Anwar kicks off second day of Johor campaign with Layang‑Layang breakfast meet‑and‑greet
items.summary: KLUANG, July 5 — On entering the second day of the 16th Johor state election campaign series, Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today spent time mingling with the local community in the ‘PMX Santai Sarapan’ programme in Simpang Renggam near here. In these polls, PH is fielding candidates in all 56 state seats consisting of 20 PKR, Amanah (19) and DAP (17) representatives. Overall, the Johor Polls is seeing a total of 172 candidates contesting for the 56 seats with the Election Commission (EC) setting July 11 as polling day, while early voting is on July 7.

items.title: Three men killed in 4WD collision at ‘P’ turning on Pan Borneo Sarikei-Sibu stretch
items.summary: KUCHING, July 5 — Three men were killed while another suffered minor injuries in a crash involving two four-wheel drive (4WD) vehicles at a ‘P’ turning along the Pan Borneo Sarikei-Sibu Highway last night. “Firefighters found that the crash involved a single cab and a double cab 4WD vehicle. The driver of the single cab vehicle suffered minor injuries,” the department said in a statement last night.

items.title: Anwar steps up Pakatan campaign momentum with eight‑seat tour in northern Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 5 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday intensifies the coalition’s campaign momentum for the Johor state election in a packed tour across eight constituencies in the northern part of the state to strengthen public support for its candidates and machinery. In a post on his Facebook page, yesterday’s tour will see him engaging with voters in Layang-Layang, Senggarang, Semerah, Bukit Naning, Pemanis, Gambir, Serom, and Palong Timur, which fall under the Buloh Kasap state legislative assembly (DUN) seat. Overall, the Johor state election sees a total of 172 candidates contesting, with polling day set for July 11 and early voting on July 7.

items.title: Anwar says he wants to leave a legacy of fair, clean leadership
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday reaffirmed his commitment to steer Malaysia towards becoming a great and successful nation. As Prime Minister, I must serve everybody. Tomorrow when I retire, I want people to say Anwar loved the people, and the people loved him back.
